verdict.answer_first: B: opens with the concrete judgment, "It's a data-leak bug waiting to happen"; A also answers first with "cross-request data leak waiting to happen."
verdict.decisiveness: B commits cleanly to "thread the value explicitly" and names AsyncLocalStorage only as the fallback; A is also decisive but spends more space expanding alternatives.
verdict.fabrication: none; both stay within grounded implications of module-level mutable request state, though their async examples are hypothetical.
verdict.actionability: A gives the most immediately copyable fix plus an AsyncLocalStorage code sketch; B is cleaner but less complete for implementation.
